frameworks.name: grey-box linear modeling
frameworks.one_line: data-driven linearization of a physiological model fit to fNIRS signals

frameworks.name: multi-compartmental neurovascular unit model
frameworks.one_line: nonlinear physiological model linearized to identify tDCS pathway

frameworks.name: Akaike information criterion
frameworks.one_line: model selection criterion for choosing appropriate linear complexity

core_question: Through which neurovascular pathway does anodal HD-tDCS evoke the cerebrovascular reactivity observed by fNIRS?
key_assumptions: neurovascular unit can be decomposed into vascular smooth muscle, perivascular space, synaptic space, and astrocyte compartments
nonlinear NVU dynamics admit a linear approximation adequate for fNIRS CVR fitting
early (first 150 s) anodal HD-tDCS response reflects pathway-specific perturbation
manipulations: candidate tDCS perturbation pathway (including perivascular potassium / Pathway 3)
linear model complexity selected by AIC
anodal HD-tDCS applied at targeted region in healthy humans
key_findings: grey-box linear model fits fNIRS CVR to first 150 s of anodal HD-tDCS across eleven participants
Pathway 3 (perivascular potassium concentration) yields the lowest mean square error and lowest AIC
interpretations: anodal HD-tDCS evoked hemodynamic response is best explained by perturbation via perivascular potassium
